req.title: Reset employee password — variant 3
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-104 has four testable axes: (1) happy-path reset with a valid token, (2) rejection of an expired token (>30 min), (3) boundary at exactly the 30-minute expiry mark, (4) password-policy violation, and (5) webhook emission. EP covers valid vs invalid token classes; BVA targets the 30-minute window edge; STC traces the token lifecycle states (active→used, active→expired); NEG covers the policy-failure path; DT captures the combined condition table for token validity × password policy. Five tests are generated to satisfy the minimum three and provide meaningful coverage.

tests.name: test_reset_password_valid_token_success
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists in the database for the target employee
The hr-app service is reachable at the configured base URL
A webhook listener fixture is active and can capture events
actions: POST /password/reset with the valid token and a policy-compliant new password
Capture the HTTP response status and body
Query the webhook listener for the emitted event
Attempt to authenticate with the old password to verify session invalidation
expected: All existing sessions for the employee are invalidated
A webhook event with type 'password.reset' is received by the listener
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_valid_token_success(
    api_client,          # <fixture> pre-configured requests.Session
    valid_reset_token,   # <fixture> fresh token < 30 min old
    webhook_listener,    # <fixture> captures POST events
    old_session_token,   # <fixture> active session before reset
    compliant_password,  # <fixture> e.g. 'T3st!P@ssw0rd'
):
    payload = {"token": valid_reset_token, "password": compliant_password}
    resp = api_client.post("/password/reset", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "updated"

    # Verify session invalidation
    session_check = api_client.get(
        "/me", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {old_session_token}"}
    )
    assert session_check.status_code == 401, "Old session should be invalidated"

    # Verify webhook
    event = webhook_listener.latest_event(event_type="password.reset")
    assert event is not None, "Webhook event not received"
    assert event["employee_id"] == <employee_id_fixture>

tests.name: test_reset_password_expired_token_rejected
preconditions: A reset token that was issued more than 30 minutes ago exists in the database
actions: POST /password/reset with the expired token and a policy-compliant new password
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating the link has expired
Password is NOT changed in the database
trace: R-WEB-104: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_expired_token_rejected(
    api_client,           # <fixture>
    expired_reset_token,  # <fixture> token created > 30 min ago
    compliant_password,   # <fixture>
    webhook_listener,     # <fixture>
    get_employee_password_hash,  # <helper_fn>
    employee_id,          # <fixture>
):
    original_hash = get_employee_password_hash(employee_id)

    payload = {"token": expired_reset_token, "password": compliant_password}
    resp = api_client.post("/password/reset", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "expired" in body.get("error", "").lower()

    # Password must not have changed
    current_hash = get_employee_password_hash(employee_id)
    assert current_hash == original_hash, "Password was mutated by expired token"

    # No webhook expected
    event = webhook_listener.latest_event(event_type="password.reset")
    assert event is None, "Webhook must NOT fire for expired token"

tests.name: test_reset_password_token_at_30_minute_boundary
preconditions: Two reset tokens are prepared: one aged exactly 29m59s (should succeed) and one aged exactly 30m00s (should be rejected)
actions: POST /password/reset with the 29m59s token and a compliant password
POST /password/reset with the 30m00s token and a compliant password
expected: Boundary is enforced at exactly 30 minutes
trace: R-WEB-104: email link expires after 30 minutes
R-WEB-104: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_token_at_30_minute_boundary(
    api_client,
    make_reset_token_aged,   # <helper_fn>(seconds) -> token string
    compliant_password,
    alt_compliant_password,  # <fixture> different valid password
):
    token_just_valid = make_reset_token_aged(seconds=1799)   # 29m59s
    token_just_expired = make_reset_token_aged(seconds=1800) # 30m00s

    resp_valid = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token_just_valid, "password": compliant_password},
    )
    assert resp_valid.status_code == 200, (
        f"29m59s token must succeed, got {resp_valid.status_code}"
    )

    resp_expired = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token_just_expired, "password": alt_compliant_password},
    )
    assert resp_expired.status_code == 410, (
        f"30m00s token must be rejected, got {resp_expired.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_reset_password_policy_violation_rejected
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists
The hr-app enforces a password policy (min length, complexity, etc.)
actions: POST /password/reset with the valid token and a non-compliant password (e.g. too short, no special chars)
Capture HTTP response status and validation errors
expected: Response body lists password policy violations
Password is NOT changed in the database
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_password,expected_error_fragment", [
    ("short",         "length"),
    ("alllowercase1",  "uppercase"),
    ("ALLUPPERCASE1",  "lowercase"),
    ("NoDigitsHere!",  "digit"),
])
def test_reset_password_policy_violation_rejected(
    api_client,
    valid_reset_token,
    bad_password,
    expected_error_fragment,
    webhook_listener,
    get_employee_password_hash,
    employee_id,
):
    original_hash = get_employee_password_hash(employee_id)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": valid_reset_token, "password": bad_password},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    errors = str(body.get("errors", "")).lower()
    assert expected_error_fragment in errors, (
        f"Expected '{expected_error_fragment}' in errors: {errors}"
    )

    assert get_employee_password_hash(employee_id) == original_hash
    assert webhook_listener.latest_event("password.reset") is None

tests.name: test_reset_password_token_state_transition
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists (state: ACTIVE)
actions: POST /password/reset with the valid token and a compliant password (ACTIVE -> USED)
POST /password/reset again with the SAME token (USED -> rejected)
expected: First reset returns HTTP 200 and updates the password
Second reset with the same token returns HTTP 410 (token already consumed)
Token transitions: ACTIVE -> USED, subsequent use is rejected
trace: R-WEB-104: valid reset token (state transition from active to consumed)
R-WEB-104: expired/used links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_token_state_transition(
    api_client,
    valid_reset_token,
    compliant_password,
    alt_compliant_password,
    webhook_listener,
):
    # Transition: ACTIVE -> USED
    resp_first = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": valid_reset_token, "password": compliant_password},
    )
    assert resp_first.status_code == 200, (
        f"First use must succeed, got {resp_first.status_code}"
    )

    # Verify webhook fired once
    event = webhook_listener.latest_event("password.reset")
    assert event is not None, "Webhook must fire on first use"
    webhook_listener.reset()  # <helper_fn> clears captured events

    # Transition: USED -> rejected (replay attempt)
    resp_second = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": valid_reset_token, "password": alt_compliant_password},
    )
    assert resp_second.status_code == 410, (
        f"Replay of used token must return 410, got {resp_second.status_code}"
    )

    # No second webhook
    assert webhook_listener.latest_event("password.reset") is None
